arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou appears to have been on the end of some criticism of late, with Tottenham’s 6-3 defeat at the hands of Liverpool doing the Australian no favours whatsoever. arts.ttl: Newcastle and Tottenham to battle European giants for €35m-rated talent wanted by nine other clubs
arts.sum: Decent centre-backs don’t come up for sale very often, so it’s no surprise to see up to 11 clubs wanted to do battle for PSG’s brilliant Slovak defender, Milan Skriniar. arts.ttl: Former Tottenham & Everton Star Set to Join Como on Trial Basis
arts.sum: Former Tottenham Hotspur star Dele Alli is on the verge of joining Como on a trial basis after terminating his contract at Everton last summer. According to Gazzetta dello Sport, the 28-year-old midfielder will train with Cesc Fabregas' side for the first time today. arts.ttl: Tottenham prepared to blow London rivals out of the water for exciting Juve talent
arts.sum: Juventus’ young Italian footballer Nicolo Fagioli continues to attract the interest of many Premier League clubs. arts.ttl: Ange Postecoglou admits big Tottenham wishlist amid Ben Godfrey January transfer update
arts.sum: Asked how realistic it is that there are players available in January to improve the team and also improve the depth of the squad, the Australian revealed that the new format for UEFA's club competitions could possibly complicate matters this time around. Just like Postecoglou, Espirito Santo also came in for criticism during his time in N17 but the current Spurs boss believes that it all stems from people looking for perfection.

arts.ttl: What Ange Postecoglou will ask Daniel Levy for in the January transfer window at Tottenham
arts.sum: As always Tottenham must continue to keep within their squad limits and the rules for homegrown and foreign players, with the Europa League lacking in spaces for new recruits unless others drop out, but Postecoglou will need to continue his squad revamp in this coming January window with at least one or two arrivals to prevent another period of struggle and overused players. When football.london put it to Postecoglou toward the end of the last summer window that he had swapped Eric Dier for Radu Dragusin and last season admitted he wanted another centre-back, so surely needed another natural man in the middle of the back four, the Australian played down the idea.
